blockerReason: Canvas-side Penpot MCP Plugin not running. MCP server is reachable and the mcp__penpot__* tool surface is loaded into the session, but execute_code and export_shape are routed by the server to a plugin instance that must be active inside an open Penpot design file and paired with the operator's user token. Server returned a structured 'No plugin instance connected for user token' error on the first no-mutation probe. Until plugin install + pairing happens in Penpot, no design read or write is possible.
manualActionsRequested: Open Penpot in browser; open (or create) the InsuranceAIPlatform design file.
Open the Plugins panel and install/enable the Penpot MCP Plugin if not already present.
Launch the plugin inside the open file so it pairs with the MCP server via the user token.
Verify the plugin shows Connected; send trigger phrase 'Penpot plugin connected' (or 'Penpot ready' again).
nextSafeStep: On trigger the implementer re-probes (getPages + shapeStructure(root, 2)), creates the 'MCP Access Test — InsuranceAIPlatform' frame near origin, calls mcp__penpot__export_shape to publish a PNG under latest-screens/penpot-access-test-2026-05-24.png, and republishes with PENPOT_ACCESS_TEST_PASSED / PARTIAL / BLOCKED.

figmaFileMoveAndScreenshot.note: independent — awaits operator manual UI actions in Figma (access check on Team project, move/duplicate from Drafts, PNG export of dashboard frame)
